title: Why a Cancer Drug That Restores Differentiation Is Better Than One That Kills
color_semantics: TEAL = differentiated/mature cell/drug working/epigenetic lock released; CRIMSON = immature blast/2HG present/epigenetic lock engaged

metadata.note: EXCLUSIONS: no IDH biochemistry beyond 2HG-as-competitive-inhibitor concept; no comparison of IDH1 vs IDH2 inhibitors; no distinction between AML and glioma IDH mutations; no DNMT inhibitor mechanism; no discussion of EZH2 inhibitors

beats.narration_text: A leukemia patient starts a new drug. Weeks later the blast count falls. The cancer cells did not die.

props.headline: On the blood smear: mature neutrophils with segmented nuclei — cells…

beats.narration_text: The blood smear shows mature neutrophils with segmented nuclei — a cell type that should not be present at this stage in this bone marrow.
Those neutrophils carry the original IDH1 mutation. They were the cancer. They completed differentiation.
Cancer drugs are designed to kill cancer cells. Here blast counts fall without cell death — cells mature instead. How does blocking a metabolic enzyme produce terminal differentiation?
Normal blood development is unidirectional: a stem cell commits, matures through defined stages, and reaches a terminal state. Maturation is the endpoint.
In AML, that program stalls. Blasts — immature progenitors — accumulate because the differentiation gate is locked.
The IDH1 mutation produces a metabolite called 2-hydroxyglutarate — 2HG. Structurally, 2HG resembles the substrate that alpha-ketoglutarate-dependent demethylases require to erase methyl marks.
2HG competitively inhibits the demethylases. Methyl marks that must be erased to open differentiation gene promoters remain. The epigenetic lock holds and maturation cannot proceed.
Ivosidenib blocks the mutant IDH1 enzyme. 2HG levels fall. Demethylase activity recovers. Methyl marks on differentiation gene promoters are gradually erased.

props.headline: The cell was always going to differentiate. The program was intact.…

beats.narration_text: The differentiation program was intact throughout. The 2HG-mediated epigenetic lock was the sole barrier. Remove the lock — the program runs.
Illustrative timeline: day 0 — eighty percent blasts. Day 14 — bilobed nuclei appear. Day 28 — mature neutrophils refill the marrow. Day 56 — blast count below five percent.
A metabolic inhibitor removed an oncometabolite-imposed epigenetic lock. The developmental program resumed. The cancer did not die — it finished maturing.

beats.name: OutroSeries
beats.narration_text: This is part of the Cancer Biology series from Humanitarians AI.

props.tagline: AI education for humanitarian and social-impact practitioners.
